text: Re-vendored engine bundles from vfkb commit fea3895 (vilosource/vfkb PR #76): storage.defaultProject() replaces the hard-coded 'spike' project label — derived from the brain dir (<repo>/.vfkb → repo), else $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR, else cwd; VFKB_PROJECT still wins. Plugin version 0.1.0 → 0.1.1. Verified the VENDORED copy in a sandbox: VFKB_DATA_DIR=<sandbox>/fakerepo/.vfkb renders <vfkb-resume project="fakerepo">.

Re-vendored engine bundles from vfkb v2 SHIPPED main (vilosource/vfkb@5bb087e, the v2->main ship merge PR #86) — plugin v0.2.0. Brings the v2 engine to plugin consumers: session backbone (ADR-0039, hooks read session_id from stdin), native concurrency lock (ADR-0040), schema honesty / read-boundary zod validation + refs.contradicts (ADR-0042), storage seam (ADR-0044). Note vfkb.mjs grew ~48KB->~628KB (zod now bundled into the CLI, expected post-ADR-0042). Sandbox-verified the VENDORED copies: CLI add+resume renders project=fakerepo; MCP tools/list=9. Evidence upstream: vfkb suite 199/199 on the ship head; full L4 re-pin pi 32/33 / claude 31/32 (vfkb PR #84).

v0.4.0 SHIPPED-CANDIDATE (2026-07-09): engine re-vendored from vfkb main@7c20d4d (ADR-0049 last-handoff pin, adversarial-review hardened: 2k truncation cap, total-order tie-break, constitutional dedup) + Layer 1 built here: /vfkb:brief skill (skills/brief) forking into the Haiku-pinned briefer agent (agents/briefer.md, model: haiku per official sub-agents docs). VERIFIED: vendored CLI renders ## Last handoff + MCP tools/list=9 (sandbox); /vfkb:brief verified END-TO-END via claude --plugin-dir sandboxes, wired arm named the seeded sentinel next-step exactly with all 5 template sections + graceful gh degradation, contrast arm (no handoff) reported UNKNOWN without fabricating. Unverified: that the fork actually ran on haiku (not observable from -p output; frontmatter contract doc-verified).

/vfkb:brief L4 formalized + release-gate Brake (2026-07-09, closes the v0.4.0 shipped-unverified violation): scenarios/brief-skill.mjs DEMONSTRATED 3/3 vs 0/3 — each wired hit requires the sentinel AND haiku OBSERVED in claude -p --output-format json modelUsage (outer pinned to sonnet; observed: ONLY haiku sampled, the fork consumed the whole path). scenarios/release-gate.mjs + GH Actions: PRs fail unless every REQUIRED record is demonstrated:true AND record.pluginVersion === plugin.json version — bumping the version without re-running the L4 goes red deterministically. Negative-checked (gate fails on synthetic 0.5.0 bump). Per vfkb ADR-0050: prose DoD rules get skipped; the Brake cannot be.

v0.5.0 RELEASED (merged to main 2026-07-12, PR #12): re-vendored engine bundles from vfkb main@c73cf8e. Carries: the vfkb#127 MCP tags fix (JSON-array-string coercion, loud garbage rejection, kb_supersede tags), vfkb --version incl. the bundle-layout crash fix (vfkb PR#143, caught by THIS re-vendor's smoke check), doctor npm-currency + stale-clone detection, CLI flag hardening, export agents-md/okf. Evidence: brief-skill L4 re-run against 0.5.0 DEMONSTRATED (wired 3/3 sentinel+haiku-observed, contrast 0/3); release-gate PASSED (local + CI). Delivery remains UNPROVEN per DELIVERY-STATUS.json (install-path L4 still open).

This repo IS fully vfkb-wired (verified 2026-07-16): plugin-wired via .claude/settings.json (enabledPlugins vfkb@vfkb + guard hook), .claude/vfkb-guard.mjs committed, .vfkb brain committed, and a project-scoped install record exists (v0.5.0) so vfkb runs live here. Added CLAUDE.md orientation: ADRs/RFCs live in the vfkb repo (~/VFKB/vfkb/docs/adr), releases are hand-cut + tagged vfkb--v{version} (ADR-0060), delivery unproven until scenarios/records/install-path.json (ADR-0051). No bootstrap script needed — plugin-wired, not the legacy VFKB_BUNDLE_DIR path.

ADR-0061 ACCEPTED (2026-07-16): plugin bump-and-tag is now enforced by two deterministic mechanisms, not prose. INVARIANT: a shipped version is immutable — if the consumer-facing surface (plugin/ + templates/) differs from what vfkb--v{version} already shipped, the version is stale and must be bumped. Mechanisms: (1) scenarios/version-bump.mjs — PR Brake, diffs the surface against the TAG (not the merge-base, so it asks "does the artifact match the version it claims?" and holds on any checkout); ships version-bump.selftest.mjs, 10 cases on real git repos, all observed going red/green as required. (2) .github/workflows/release-tag.yml — on merge to main, re-runs the deterministic gates then creates+pushes the annotated vfkb--v{version} at that commit; "claude plugin tag" leaves the release procedure. Kept OUT of release-gate.mjs to preserve that gate's pure-filesystem property. No skip label (ADR-0050). Tag format vfkb--v{version} preserved and asserted before push; ADR-0051 delivery status untouched (still unproven).

KEY CORRECTION TO ADR-0060 (observed, not asserted): ADR-0060 claims v0.5.0 drifted by shipping the INACTIVE guard (#16) AND the hooks-smoke L4 (#15). Reading the four commits 0fc7a88..e7550fd — NONE touched plugin/. The real drift is exactly ONE file: templates/vfkb-guard.mjs (#16), which consumers copy+commit (ADR-0059). #15 touched only scenarios/ + RELEASING.md and changed no consumer byte — it was never drift. This is load-bearing: the obvious Brake ("if plugin/** changed, require a bump") would have been GREEN on the very commit that motivated it. Hence surface = plugin/ + templates/. VERIFIED by replaying the real history (tag v0.5.0 at the bump 0fc7a88, check tip e7550fd) → Brake goes red naming templates/vfkb-guard.mjs.

release-please REJECTED on three source-verified grounds (googleapis/release-please@5d70353 v17.10.3, the version release-please-action@v5.0.0 resolves): (a) it cannot parse back the tag it emits — TAG_PATTERN's greedy .* makes vfkb--v0.5.0 round-trip to component "vfkb-" not "vfkb" (ran the real regex myself), so it would emit our tags then fail to recognize its own releases, treating every run as beginning-of-time; multi-char separators are untested upstream. (b) chore: is hidden by default and base.ts skips releases on an empty changelog — 4 of our 6 shipped versions are "chore: re-vendor", so it would have cut ZERO. (c) its release PR is force-pushed with a hardcoded, non-configurable force:true that rebuilds the branch from main's head — the bot's bump PR is born red (records still bound to the old version) and any L4 records added to it are destroyed on the next run. The standing release PR is structurally incompatible with this repo's evidence discipline.

ACCEPTED TRADE-OFF, stated plainly: surface changes now cost 3 metered L4 re-runs and that cost is no longer evadable — drifting the version was the (unintentional) way around the gate's version-bound record rule. This makes an existing cost honest rather than adding one, but a one-line templates/ fix is now a release. v0.5.0's drift stays blessed (ADR-0060 retro-tagged at the tip); the invariant holds FORWARD.

Why: ADR-0060 declared bump-and-tag "one atomic step" but left both halves hand-typed, and both were skipped within days (ADR-0050: prose DoD rules get skipped; a Brake cannot be). Enforcement had to be mechanical. release-please is right for the vfkb engine repo and wrong here — it would silently break the mandatory tag format, cut zero of our chore-driven re-vendor releases, and clobber the metered evidence the release gate requires.
